mei.relationship: Same mansion, same frequency — candidate and company are in sync with no external forces at play
mei.daily_feel: Candidate adapts naturally with low communication cost, almost no integration time needed
mei.energy_flow_desc: Bidirectional and balanced — neither party drains the other
mei.long_term_risk: Too comfortable may lead to stagnation; team lacks growth stimulus
mei.growth: Low. Company won't push the candidate to grow, and the candidate won't bring new stimulus
mei.stability: High. The balance of mei provides long-term stability with low turnover risk
mei.bad_year_impact: Bad years don't create additional drain from the company; impact is minimal
mei.fit_for: Teams needing stability and consistency; best when the candidate has strong self-motivation

hr.relationship: Same mansion, candidate and company frequencies aligned
hr.daily_feel: Zero integration cost; no special onboarding support needed
hr.bad_year_impact: No additional retention risk during bad years
hr.fit_for: Culture-fit-priority positions; most effective when the candidate is highly self-driven

gyo.relationship: Past-life karma — candidate naturally carries a sense of mission to drive the company's objectives
gyo.daily_feel: Candidate invests effort voluntarily without special motivation needed
gyo.energy_flow_desc: Candidate outputs slightly, but karmic support prevents excessive fatigue
gyo.long_term_risk: Gyo party tends to over-invest and neglect personal needs; HR should proactively monitor work-life balance
gyo.growth: Medium. Candidate grows through driving company objectives, but direction may not align with their own preferences
gyo.stability: Medium-high. Karmic relationship provides underlying stability; retention is reasonable
gyo.bad_year_impact: Bad years make the sense of mission heavier but won't cause collapse. Moderate pressure relief from HR is sufficient
gyo.fit_for: Teams with long-term goals needing mission-driven talent

hr.relationship: Candidate has a natural drive to advance company objectives; no additional motivation needed
hr.daily_feel: Candidate self-invests but may neglect their own needs
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad years increase mission burden; HR should proactively relieve pressure
hr.fit_for: Long-term planning roles; managers need to regularly check on work-life balance

tai.relationship: Karmic receiver — the company naturally provides care and support to the candidate
tai.daily_feel: Candidate settles in quickly with a sense of being supported
tai.energy_flow_desc: Company provides slight output; candidate is the receiver
tai.long_term_risk: Candidate may become overly dependent on company support, reducing self-reliance
tai.growth: Medium. Passive growth, following the team's pace
tai.stability: Medium-high. Karmic support exists but full dependence isn't sustainable
tai.bad_year_impact: Company acts as a buffer during bad years; less impact than going it alone
tai.fit_for: Teams with strong training systems; best when the candidate accepts guidance well

hr.relationship: Company naturally provides support; candidate receives smoothly
hr.daily_feel: Fast onboarding speed with short adaptation period
hr.bad_year_impact: Company acts as buffer during bad years; low retention risk
hr.fit_for: Teams with mentor systems; suited for entry-to-mid-level talent needing guidance

ei.relationship: Candidate and company mutually invest resources, creating a positive growth cycle
ei.daily_feel: Candidate's contributions are reciprocated; team also benefits — a win-win
ei.energy_flow_desc: Candidate actively invests; company feedback prevents candidate fatigue
ei.long_term_risk: If company-side feedback stops, candidate experiences sudden emptiness and turnover intent spikes
ei.growth: High. Bidirectional improvement — the best talent development environment
ei.stability: Medium-high. Stable as long as the positive cycle continues; deteriorates quickly if interrupted
ei.bad_year_impact: Bad years may weaken the cycle but won't reverse it into drain
ei.fit_for: Teams with strong feedback systems; organizations committed to mutual investment

hr.relationship: Candidate and company form a bidirectional value-creation cycle
hr.daily_feel: Candidate is productive and fulfilled; team morale trends positive
hr.bad_year_impact: Cycle weakens during bad years but doesn't reverse; maintaining basic feedback stabilizes retention
hr.fit_for: Key positions on the core team; company must maintain feedback mechanisms for retention

shin.relationship: Company invests resources to develop the candidate; candidate is the beneficiary
shin.daily_feel: Candidate feels valued and nurtured; quick to settle into the role
shin.long_term_risk: Candidate may become overly dependent on company mentoring, lacking independence after departure
shin.growth: High. Passive but effective growth; candidate's ability to internalize is key
shin.stability: High. Shin position provides stable benefits; retention is strong
shin.bad_year_impact: Bad years may reduce investment effectiveness but won't reverse into draining the candidate
shin.fit_for: Companies willing to invest in talent development; highest ROI when candidate has strong learning ability

hr.relationship: Company invests in development; candidate quickly absorbs and grows
hr.daily_feel: Candidate has a sense of belonging and growth; satisfaction is high
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad years weaken development effects but won't trigger departure
hr.fit_for: Positions with clear training budgets and defined career paths

yu.relationship: Candidate is the giver — team draws atmosphere and social energy from them
yu.daily_feel: Candidate drives team relationships proactively, but fatigue isn't easily noticed
yu.energy_flow_desc: Candidate continuously outputs social energy; team receives. Candidate's fatigue may go undetected
yu.long_term_risk: Effective at the social layer only; candidate's professional depth needs separate attention
yu.growth: Medium. Social skills grow but professional depth requires separate arrangements
yu.stability: Medium. Candidate's sense of over-giving accumulates; periodic recharging needed
yu.bad_year_impact: Bad years reduce candidate's social energy; team atmosphere may decline accordingly
yu.fit_for: Organizations valuing team culture, but managers must proactively care for candidate's energy state

hr.relationship: Candidate is the team's social lubricant, but drain isn't easily visible
hr.daily_feel: Candidate energizes the atmosphere, but managers should proactively watch for fatigue signals
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad years reduce social energy; manager should step in to sustain team morale
hr.fit_for: Teams with employee care mechanisms; manager consciously reciprocates the candidate's contributions

sui.relationship: Company is the yu party investing actively, but candidate is the sui party being continuously drained
sui.daily_feel: Company provides resources and care, but candidate's capacity is limited; fatigue accumulates gradually
sui.energy_flow_desc: Company invests heavily; candidate's returns become strained. First year is the peak, then declining
sui.long_term_risk: Sui party 'should not relocate or travel' — drained to the point of wanting to leave but unable to
sui.growth: High but passive. Company pushes candidate's growth, but candidate has no say in direction
sui.stability: Medium-low. The comfort of yusui diminishes over time; fatigue accumulates
sui.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus sui party equals double drain; turnover risk is extremely high
sui.fit_for: Short-term contracts are safer than long-term hires; can extend observation if candidate has strong stress tolerance

hr.relationship: Company investment is high but candidate is the drained party; long-term retention risk exists
hr.daily_feel: Initial performance is strong, but output declines after six months; manager should be prepared
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad years are the turnover peak; HR must prepare succession plans in advance
hr.fit_for: Short-term contracts with clear exit mechanisms, or candidates with proven stress tolerance

an.relationship: Candidate leads team stability but must continuously output energy to maintain it
an.daily_feel: Candidate holds the umbrella — stabilizes the situation while the team depends on their steady output
an.energy_flow_desc: Candidate continuously outputs to maintain stability; team draws security from it
an.long_term_risk: An party's continuous output goes unnoticed; by the time fatigue hits, they're already overdrawn
an.growth: Medium. Management skills grow, but candidate gets locked into the stabilizer role
an.stability: Surface high, actual drain. Stable while candidate holds up; collapses when they can't
an.bad_year_impact: Bad years double the stabilization pressure; candidate's departure or burnout risk increases
an.fit_for: Suited when candidate is skilled in management and can set boundaries; otherwise they'll be drained

hr.relationship: Candidate is the pillar of team stability, but drain is hidden
hr.daily_feel: Team appears stable on the surface, but candidate's burnout isn't easily visible
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad years double the stabilization pressure; HR must proactively intervene to reduce load
hr.fit_for: Management positions with clear boundary-setting and rotation mechanisms

kai.relationship: Company continuously extracts energy from the candidate — 'nothing else is bearable'
kai.daily_feel: Not noticeable at first; after six months, nothing the candidate does feels sufficient
kai.energy_flow_desc: Company absorbs the candidate's stability; candidate's energy drains one-way
kai.long_term_risk: 'Nothing else is bearable' — after 2-3 years it becomes very difficult
kai.growth: Low. All energy is being extracted; no capacity left for growth
kai.stability: Low. Kai party is the most unstable position; departure could happen anytime
kai.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus kai party equals high turnover risk — potentially the career lowest point
kai.fit_for: Long-term hiring is not recommended. If necessary, use short-term contracts with exit mechanisms

hr.relationship: Company continuously drains candidate's energy; lowest retention rate of all pairings
hr.daily_feel: Candidate's output starts declining after six months; visible signs of strain
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus kai party means near-certain departure; HR must arrange succession in advance
hr.fit_for: Short-term contract projects only; absolutely not for critical positions requiring stability

ki.relationship: Company applies pressure to drive candidate's growth — a pressure-based interaction model
ki.daily_feel: Candidate feels challenged; stimulating short-term but fatiguing long-term
ki.energy_flow_desc: Company applies pressure; candidate absorbs. Drain isn't low but there's growth return
ki.long_term_risk: Ki party's pressure tolerance has limits; beyond that, growth becomes harm
ki.growth: High but with a cost. Pressure-driven growth that needs adequate rest mechanisms
ki.stability: Medium. Stable pressure provides good stimulus; excessive pressure causes collapse
ki.bad_year_impact: Bad year pressure may crush rather than push the candidate; requires special attention
ki.fit_for: Suited when candidate has strong stress tolerance and self-regulation; company should provide rest opportunities

hr.relationship: Company drives candidate growth through pressure; precise calibration of pressure intensity is required
hr.daily_feel: Candidate's short-term growth is visible, but manager must watch for the fatigue tipping point
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year pressure may overwhelm the candidate; HR should provide stress relief support
hr.fit_for: Teams with strong pressure management and leave policies

sei.relationship: Candidate grows through friction with the company; becomes increasingly stronger after adaptation
sei.daily_feel: Candidate is uncomfortable initially; after adapting, they realize they've become stronger
sei.energy_flow_desc: Company's characteristics refine the candidate; candidate passively strengthens with low drain
sei.long_term_risk: Sei party risk is low, but an initial adaptation period is needed
sei.growth: High. Growth after friction is solid and lasting
sei.stability: High. Sei party is one of the most stable positions after the adjustment period
sei.bad_year_impact: Bad years intensify the friction feeling but won't cause collapse
sei.fit_for: Best when the candidate can tolerate initial discomfort and take a long-term view

hr.relationship: Candidate grows steadily through friction; later-stage performance keeps improving
hr.daily_feel: Patience needed early on; clear progress visible after three months
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad years increase friction intensity but don't affect retention willingness
hr.fit_for: Teams willing to give the candidate adaptation time; high long-term returns
